claim_ledger_record.claim_text: Claim ledgers preserve claim identity across reader-facing paraphrases when scope and support state are unchanged.

revision_history.reason: The reader-facing wording is treated as a ledger variant because scope and support-state effect remain unchanged.

promotion_blockers: semantic-equivalence review preserves argument support only
review_routes: human semantic-equivalence review before any support-state movement
residuals: Equivalent variants are merged only for ledger identity; semantic equivalence remains review-scoped and does not promote support.

claim_text_variants.text: Reasoning should maintain claim ledgers with support states, provenance, contradiction links, uncertainty, and revision history.
Claim ledgers should maintain support states, provenance, contradiction links, uncertainty, and revision history for material claims.

non_claims: This semantic-variant fixture does not promote any support state.
This semantic-variant fixture does not prove semantic equivalence, source interpretation, runtime behavior, verifier quality, or belief-engine correctness.
This synthetic fixture does not promote Appendix C support states.
This synthetic fixture does not prove semantic equivalence, source interpretation, runtime behavior, verifier quality, open-domain claim extraction, or AI safety.
